lede: A look at May 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Elena Huelva and Park So-jin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Greenfield tornado
events.desc: The Greenfield tornado kills five and injures 35 across rural Iowa, United States. Wind speeds in excess of 480 kilometres per hour (300 mph) are estimated from measurements for the third time in history.

events.title: 2024 Taichung MRT attack
events.desc: A stabbing spree on the Green line of the Taichung MRT injures four people, including the perpetrator.

events.title: 2014 Taipei Metro attack
events.desc: Random killings occur on the Bannan Line of the Taipei MRT, killing four and injuring 24.

events.title: Roller coaster
events.desc: The tallest roller coaster in the world, Kingda Ka opens at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson Township, New Jersey.

events.title: Suharto
events.desc: President Suharto of Indonesia resigns following the killing of students from Trisakti University earlier that week by security forces and growing mass protests in Jakarta against his ongoing corrupt rule.

events.title: Doktor Faust
events.desc: The opera Doktor Faust, unfinished when composer Ferruccio Busoni died, is premiered in Dresden.

events.title: University of Chicago
events.desc: University of Chicago students Richard Loeb and Nathan Leopold, Jr. murder 14-year-old Bobby Franks in a "thrill killing".

events.title: Commonwealth War Graves Commission
events.desc: The Imperial War Graves Commission is established through royal charter to mark, record, and maintain the graves and places of commemoration of the British Empire's military forces.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Union Army succeeds in closing off the last escape route from Port Hudson, Louisiana, in preparation for the coming siege.

events.title: Daniel Defoe
events.desc: Daniel Defoe is imprisoned on charges of seditious libel.

births.name: Chakravarthi V. Narasimhan
births.line: Indian Civil Service Officer and former Under Secretary-General of the UN (died 2003)

deaths.text: Kyle Busch, American race car driver and owner
Otis Clark, American butler and preacher, survivor of the Tulsa race riot
Les Aspin, American captain and politician, 18th United States Secretary of Defense
Rajiv Gandhi, Indian politician, 6th Prime Minister of India
Hidesaburō Ueno, Japanese agriculturalist, guardian of Hachikō
Otto von Guericke, German physicist and inventor of the Magdeburg Hemispheres

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Adílio Daronch and Manuel Gómez González
Christian feast day: Earliest day on which Corpus Christi can fall, while June 24 is the latest; held on Thursday after Trinity Sunday (often locally moved to Sunday). (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Helena of Constantinople, also known as "Feast of the Holy Great Sovereigns Constantine and Helen, Equal-to-the-Apostles." (Eastern Orthodox Church, Anglican Communion)
Christian feast day: Blessed Hemming of Turku
